rulings.Case Title: PURE ENTERTAINMENT LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED VS. SHUI KING KWON HOM et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Award Of Supplemental Attorneys Fees And Costs Following Appeal; Request For Entry Of Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 18, 2022 line 1. CROSS-DEFENDANTS DENNIS PETROU AND BRIAN UTTER'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR AN AWARD OF SUPPLEMENTAL ATTORNEYS FEES AND COSTS FOLLOWING APPEAL; REQUEST FOR ENTRY OF JUDGMENT is continued to March 8, 2022 on Court's own motion. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: WEI-SHING SU VS. KOREY ERGUR ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Plaintiff'S Motion For A Preliminary Injunction Against Defendants Koray Ergur And Byft Llc
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 18, 2022 line 4. NOTICE OF PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR A PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION AGAINST DEFENDANTS KORAY ERGUR AND BYFT LLC is DENIED without prejudice. Moving party fails to cite any authority for the proposition that the requested relief may be granted as a preliminary injunction for the Court to order the defendant to vacate the property without appropriate legal procedures for removal of occupants. Furthermore, proposed relief is so non-specific that it is not enforceable even if granted: "cease all activities that would contribute to the property's decline or decay." =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
